title: On His Seventy-Fifth Birthday
content:
I strove with none; for none was worth my strife,
Nature I loved, and next to Nature, Art;
I warmed both hands before the fire of life,
It sinks, and I am ready to depart.
meaning:
Walter Savage Landor's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On His Seventy-Fifth Birthday"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
